rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: ninne saayankaala niivu een maadtaa iddri?
rows.english: What were you doing yesterday eveining ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: ilia, makklannu noodde. rasteeli aadtaa iddru.
rows.english: No, (I didn't see you) but saw the chidren ; they were playing in the street.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: naanu manee hinde gidakke niir haaktidde.
rows.english: I was wateiing the plants in the backyard.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Was your child playing yesterday night ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: solpa hottu aadtittu. aammeele tumba hottu altittu.
rows.english: It was playing for a while and later it was crying for a longtime.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, it was having high fever.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Poor thing, it is too young.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Were you looking after your field (yourself) ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, we ourselves were looking after the field.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: What are the crops you were growing ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: goodhi, bhatta, joola mattu hatti beliitiddvi.
rows.english: We were growing wheat, paddy, maize and cotton.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Why are you not growing now ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Now we are not getting any profit.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: No, we were not growing raggi.
